rulings.Case Title: AUTOVEST COLLISION REPAIRS, INC. DBA AUTOWEST et al VS. WILLIAM D. FERREIRA ESQ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions Aw Collission
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, September 22, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT WILLIAM FERREIRA'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions Aw Collission. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is continued to October 14, 2015, to give the moving party an opportunity to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of the moving papers with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, September 22, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF RYAN LOUIE'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents; And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The unopposed Motion of Plaintiff Ryan Louie to Compel Answers to Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, and Requests for Production of Documents is GRANTED. The request for sanctions in the amount of $2,010.00 is GRANTED. The Court will sign the proposed order at the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID RAWLINGS ET AL VS. TALK FREE, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 22, 2015, Line 12, DEFENDANT TALK FREE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the accounting cause of action as to the individuals only. The accounting cause of action is well-pleaded as to Talk Free, Inc. The remainder of the demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads direct injury that is distinct from corporate injury. Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts showing that demand on the board would be futile. Plaintiff alleges particularized facts to support the fraud claim. Plaintiff also alleges sufficient wrongful conduct to support the intentional interference claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND CHOW VS. EDWIN LEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Writ Of Mandate (Control Date)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 22, 2015, Line 7, Petition For Writ Of Mandate (Control Date). Motion for declaratory and injunctive relief and petition for writ of mandate is denied. The court will deny Plaintiffs' motion and petition on two grounds. First, the Plaintiffs have failed to establish that the Defendants withheld documents from Plaintiffs and violated their duty under Gov't Code 6253.1(a) to assist Defendants in their attempt to request and to access campaign finance contribution documents. "[A]n agency is not required to have 'clairvoyant capabilities' to discover the requester's needs." Hudgins v. I.R.S. (D.C. Cir. 1985) 620 F.Supp. 19, 21. In their request, Plaintiffs made no mention of campaign finances or campaign contributions. The Mayor's Office understood the request to refer to records of meetings with elected officials and gifts and donations received by elected officials, which made sense because it is the Mayor's Office's responsibility to keep track of that information. Furthermore, because the Ethics Commission publishes campaign finance filings on the internet for all to view and download, the Mayor's Office had no reason to believe Plaintiffs were trying to obtain the campaign finance contribution records through their office. See Kowalczyk v. Dep't of Justice (D.C. Cir. 1996) 73 F.3d 386,389. Contrary to Plaintiffs' position, the Board's communications with Plaintiffs' counsel, as provided in Exhibit G, do not support the allegation that the Board understood the request to refer to campaign finance contributions. Additionally, Mayor Lee had no duty under CPRA to provide his personal copy of the Form 460. See Gov't Code 6253 subd. (e). Thus, Defendants responded to Plaintiffs' request in compliance with the CPRA. The second reason for denying Plaintiffs' motion and petition is that the court already granted Defendants' motion to strike paragraphs 13A and 14 complaint. Because the Plaintiffs may no longer litigate the issues raised in paragraphs 13A and 14 of the complaint,, they no longer have a basis to seek relief for a CPRA violation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: KUNDRY RIVERO MUJICA ET AL VS. ISMAEL BASCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel A Forensic Examination Of Plaintiffs' Cell Phones And Further Responses To Requests For Production 11, And 40-43
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, September 22, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT EF INTERNATIONAL LANGUAGE SCHOOLS, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel A Forensic Examination Of Plaintiffs' Cell Phones And Further Responses To Requests For Production 11, And 40-43. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant EF Education First's Motion to Compel a Forensic Examination of Plaintiffs' Cell Phones and Further Responses to Requests for Production 11, and 40-43 is GRANTED. Hearing required. Preliminarily, the Court does not find that plaintiffs have waived all of their privacy rights by filing this lawsuit. The Court will appoint a neutral who will determine which messages are relevant. As to the relevant messages that contain information protected by the constitutional right to privacy, the Court-appointed neutral will balance the plaintiffs' privacy rights against the defendants' right to discovery. The parties are ordered to meet and confer on the duration that the plaintiffs' mobile phones shall be detained. The Court-appointed neutral shall prepare a privilege log of messages that are relevant but withheld on the basis of privilege. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS STEFAN VS. BIANA ROYKH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery With Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Thomas Stefan
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, September 22, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANTS BIANA ROYKH, D.D.S., KAI LEI'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery With Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Thomas Stefan. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The unopposed Motion of Defendant Biana Roykh, DDS to Compel Responses to Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, and Requests for Production of Documents is GRANTED. The request for sanctions in the amount of $1,140.00 is GRANTED. The Court will sign the proposed order at the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY, AS SUBROGEE TO VS. TRITON SUBS, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 22, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT TRITON SUBS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. Paragraph 18.4 of the contract [indemnification] states that Triton Subs, Inc. agrees to indemnify Quiznos for liability as well as damages. Globe Indem. Co. v. Larkin (1944) 62 Cal.App.2d 891, 893-894, is dispositive and explains that where an indemnification provision has those dual aspects, a cause of action runs from the payment of damages. There is no allegation in the complaint regarding when plaintiff made the payments and plaintiff is not obligated to plead around a tenuous statute of limitations defense. The court grants judicial notice of the verdict but nothing in the verdict demonstrates that plaintiff cannot state a cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
